name: Globalstar

overview.value: Globalstar, Inc. is an American telecommunications company that operates a satellite constellation in low Earth orbit for satellite phone, low-speed data transmission, and Earth observation. Its second-generation constellation consists of 25 satellites, built by Thales Alenia Space and completed in February 2013, with a backup satellite added in June 2022. In April 2026, Amazon announced an agreement to acquire Globalstar and merge it into its Amazon Leo business unit, with the acquisition intended to close in 2027.

base.reason: established aggregator, single reference (audit Phase 3 backfill, 2026-07-08)

notes: Added in the audit Phase 3 backfill (2026-07-08), verify-then-add. Weekly maintenance (2026-07-13): re-fetched Wikipedia, which still states the second-generation constellation as 25 satellites with 17 new MDA/Rocket Lab-built satellites on order (unchanged from the prior fill); status field filled as 'active' from the same page's present-tense operating description. sats_planned may be stale given press reporting (Via Satellite, Nov 2025) of delays to the new MDA-built satellites, but no source states a revised planned count, so 17 was left as-is rather than estimated.

events.headline: Globalstar launches its first four satellites on a Delta 7420
Globalstar loses 12 satellites in a Zenit-2 launch failure
Globalstar launches first six second-generation satellites
Globalstar completes 24-satellite second-generation constellation with fourth launch
Apple reveals $450 million investment in infrastructure supporting Globalstar-powered Emergency SOS
Amazon agrees to acquire Globalstar for $90 per share in cash or stock
